version: V2 — enriched with Confluence page extraction, AWS cost attribution, public pricing

tools.name: Jira
tools.source: Direct access — 141 projects, 313 boards
tools.pricing_notes: Cloud Premium ~$14–17/user/mo for ~370 users. Bundled Atlassian suite.
tools.notes: Universal work system. Criticality: Red/High per Cake Engineering Tools page. Managed by Cloud services team.
tools.interview_question: 141 projects and 18,583 open issues — is anyone tracking the grooming cadence across all of these?

tools.name: Confluence
tools.source: Direct access — 165 spaces, 14,111+ pages indexed
tools.pricing_notes: Bundled with Jira licensing.
tools.notes: Active documentation culture. Criticality: Red/High. Managed by Cloud services team.
tools.interview_question: Confluence has 14,000+ pages. Is Guru also actively used, or has it been superseded?

tools.name: Bitbucket Cloud
tools.source: Direct access — 4 workspaces, 3,191 repos
tools.pricing_notes: Bundled with Atlassian. Build minutes may be separate cost.
tools.notes: Four workspaces (madmobile, syscolabs, madpayments, syscolabsconf). Criticality: Red/High. 75% of repos stale (no commits in 90d).
tools.interview_question: GitHub is also listed in the engineering tools page with a note 'Can we get rid of this?' — what's the status?

tools.name: GitHub
tools.source: Cake Engineering Tools Confluence page — listed as Red/High criticality, managed by Leads. Note: 'Can we get rid of this?'
tools.pricing_notes: May be legacy. If still paying, potential immediate savings.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: Running alongside Bitbucket. Internal page already questions whether it should be decommissioned.
tools.interview_question: Is the GitHub org still active, or has everything moved to Bitbucket? Are you still paying for it?

tools.name: AWS
tools.source: SSO access — 18 accounts
tools.pricing_notes: Consolidated billing through mm-madmobile-mgmt. Includes $58K/mo Savings Plans, $17K/mo Enterprise Support.
tools.notes: Multi-account org with ControlTower. Top accounts: Mgmt $250K, Monvia $23K, CAKE Dev $15K, Retail Prod US $12K.
tools.interview_question: The AWS Cost Savings 2025 doc identifies $7K/mo in EBS snapshot savings alone. How much of that has been captured?

tools.name: Grafana (self-hosted)
tools.source: AWS inventory — Shared Services EKS cluster. $1,813/mo in mm-shared-services.
tools.pricing_notes: OSS, no license fee. Cost is EKS + S3 storage for Mimir/Loki/Tempo data.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: 6 observability tools identified. Self-hosted Mimir (metrics), Loki (logs), Tempo (traces). Payments has its own instance.
tools.interview_question: I count 6 monitoring/observability tools in the stack. What's the consolidation plan?

tools.name: Datadog
tools.source: Confluence — 'Datadog Reference' (MenuPad), 'Concierge Core -- Datadog Integration Effort'. URL: https://app.datadoghq.com
tools.pricing_notes: APM + RUM + Log Management. Highly variable based on hosts, custom metrics, log volume.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: Running alongside self-hosted Grafana stack. Used for Real User Monitoring and Log Explorer per Confluence. Scope and contract unknown — key ask for Ana.
tools.interview_question: Datadog is running for Concierge/MenuPad alongside the Grafana stack for everything else. Is there a plan to consolidate, or do they serve different purposes?

tools.name: Nagios
tools.source: Cake Engineering Tools page — 'Nagios - Monitor and alert based on threshold values'. Managed by Cloud services team.
tools.pricing_notes: OSS or commercial. EC2 hosting cost included in AWS.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: Legacy monitoring alongside Grafana and Datadog.
tools.interview_question: Is Nagios still actively used, or has Grafana replaced it?

tools.name: Munin
tools.source: Cake Engineering Tools page — 'Munin - To monitor servers'. Low criticality. Managed by Cloud services team.
tools.pricing_notes: Open source server monitoring.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: Server monitoring that may be superseded by Grafana Mimir.

tools.name: Graylog
tools.source: Cake Engineering Tools page — 'Graylog servers'. Low criticality. Managed by Leads/Cloud services.
tools.pricing_notes: OSS or commercial log management.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: Log management alongside Loki and Datadog Log Explorer.

tools.name: DB Cacti
tools.source: Cake Engineering Tools page — 'DB Cacti - To monitor servers'. Low criticality. Managed by DB Leads.
tools.pricing_notes: Open source network/server monitoring.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: Database-focused monitoring.

tools.name: Jenkins
tools.source: AWS: 9 Jenkins instances in mm-cake-development (dev-qa-payment-jenkins, pos-db-jenkins, qa-jenkins-master, etc.)
tools.cost_detail: ~$1,500–2,500/month estimated EC2 cost (9 instances in CAKE Dev)
tools.pricing_notes: OSS, no license. Cost is EC2 compute for 9 instances (master + slaves + ZAP).
tools.notes: OVERLAP: Dual CI/CD. Criticality: Red/High per Cake Eng Tools. Managed by Leads/Cloud services. Tooling Evaluation doc explicitly compared Jenkins vs Pipelines vs GitLab — Jenkins scored lower due to 'hassle of maintaining cluster' and 'runtime conflicts'.
tools.interview_question: The tooling evaluation doc scored Jenkins lower than Bitbucket Pipelines due to cluster maintenance overhead. Is there a timeline to consolidate?

tools.name: Bitbucket Pipelines
tools.source: Bitbucket inventory — pipeline configs in repos, 87% coverage in madpayments
tools.estimated_annual_cost: Included in Atlassian + build minute overages
tools.pricing_notes: Included in Bitbucket licensing. Build minutes may incur overages.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: Dual CI/CD with Jenkins. Preferred tool per Tooling Evaluation (scored 3-4 on criteria vs Jenkins 3).

tools.name: Terraform
tools.source: AWS inventory — state buckets across multiple accounts
tools.pricing_notes: If using OSS CLI only: free. If Terraform Cloud/Enterprise: per-user pricing.
tools.notes: IaC standard. Payments uses extensively (TF state in mm-payments-prod-us).
tools.interview_question: Are you using Terraform OSS or Terraform Cloud/Enterprise? That's a meaningful cost difference.

tools.name: Cursor
tools.source: Bitbucket — .cursor/rules in 2 repos (seao, restaurant-admin-v2). Confluence — 'Design Integration with Cursor', 'API Automation using Cursor', 'Agentic Lovable Dev Flow', 'Test Automation with Cursor Tool'.
tools.pricing_notes: $20/mo Pro, $40/mo Business per seat.
tools.notes: AI space Tools page shows <5 adoption. Multiple Confluence pages show active experimentation (Figma MCP, API automation, agentic dev flow).
tools.interview_question: I saw the Cursor + Figma MCP integration doc from Tesla team and the API automation POC from Castor. How many seats does the org actually have?

tools.name: Claude / Anthropic
tools.source: Bitbucket — CLAUDE.md in 3 madpayments repos, AGENTS.md in 4 repos (cakekiosk-kdsapplauncher, ditto-sync-agent, restaurant-admin-v2, cake-pop-automation)
tools.pricing_notes: API usage-based or included in Cursor subscription.
tools.notes: 8 of 30 scanned repos have AI tooling config (26.7% adoption rate).
tools.interview_question: CLAUDE.md is in 3 payments repos and AGENTS.md in 4 others. Is this organic adoption or a team decision?

tools.name: OpenAI / ChatGPT
tools.source: AI space Tools page — listed under 'General Corporate' as AI-enabled. N8N workflows use OpenAI for AI Summary Validation and Smart Prompt Generation.
tools.pricing_notes: ChatGPT Team/Enterprise or API usage for N8N workflows.
tools.notes: Used both as corporate tool and embedded in N8N automation workflows.
tools.interview_question: Is OpenAI usage corporate-wide, or specific teams? API or seat-based?

tools.name: Wiz.io
tools.source: AWS inventory — CloudFormation stacks for Wiz deployment across accounts. Confluence: 'Wiz Critical high bug fixes' release notes.
tools.pricing_notes: Enterprise CSPM/CWPP. Per-cloud-account or flat annual contract. Likely one of the largest non-AWS line items.
tools.notes: Deployed via CloudFormation across all 18 accounts. Active security remediation (Wiz bug fix releases in Confluence). KEY ASK: Contract terms and cost for Ana request.
tools.interview_question: Wiz is deployed across all 18 AWS accounts. What's the annual contract, and who owns the vendor relationship?

tools.name: Trend Micro Cloud One
tools.source: AWS Cost Explorer — $1,437.68/month in mm-cake-development listed as 'TrendAI Cloud One'
tools.pricing_notes: Cloud workload security. Found in CAKE Development account only.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: Running alongside Wiz. May be legacy or complementary (workload protection vs CSPM). Not previously in catalog.
tools.interview_question: Trend Micro Cloud One is costing $1,400/mo in the CAKE Dev account. Is that intentional alongside Wiz?

tools.name: AWS GuardDuty
tools.source: AWS inventory — active across all 18 accounts
tools.notes: Usage-based pricing within AWS bill.

tools.name: AWS Security Hub
tools.source: AWS inventory — enabled in CAKE R&D ($11/month)
tools.notes: May not be enabled across all accounts.

tools.name: Snyk
tools.source: AWS — Snyk-related Lambda in CAKE R&D. Confluence — 'Snyk Implementation and Training' (onboarded Jan 2023, Kickoff Dec 2022). Tooling Evaluation mentions Snyk integration as a requirement.
tools.pricing_notes: Per-developer or per-repo. Enterprise pricing. Onboarded with dedicated Snyk implementation consultant.
teams_using: Unknown — onboarded in 2023 but current adoption unclear
tools.notes: Full onboarding completed in Jan 2023 with Snyk rep. But minimal recent references suggest it may be underutilized. KEY ASK: Is it still active?
tools.interview_question: Snyk was formally onboarded in Jan 2023 with a deployment workshop. Is it actively used in CI/CD pipelines today, or has it gone quiet?

tools.name: SageMaker
tools.source: AWS AI scan — 9 notebooks in CAKE Dev, 2 in R&D, 1 Studio domain, 31 AI-related S3 buckets
tools.notes: Active ML infrastructure. Notebooks suggest data science experimentation.

tools.name: AWS Bedrock
tools.source: AWS AI scan — accessible in CAKE Dev, R&D, Mgmt. Confluence 'CR - Costing' page details model pricing for Claude, Titan, Llama, Cohere, Stability AI.
tools.estimated_annual_cost: Usage-based — Confluence costing page shows ~260 tokens per prompt cycle
tools.pricing_notes: Per-token pricing. Costing page selected Claude 2.1 after model comparison.
tools.notes: Concierge AI product uses Bedrock. Costing analysis in Architecture space evaluated 6+ foundation models.
tools.interview_question: The architecture team did a detailed model comparison and chose Claude 2.1 via Bedrock. Has that been updated since? Any usage of newer models?

tools.name: Grafana (self-hosted)
tools.source: Duplicate — see entry above
tools.notes: Duplicate entry removed — see Grafana above.

tools.name: Guru
tools.source: Restaurant Update deck mentions AI-produced documentation via Guru. Access not confirmed.
tools.pricing_notes: Per-user pricing.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: May overlap with Confluence. Status unclear. KEY ASK for Ana.
tools.interview_question: Is Guru still used alongside Confluence, or has one replaced the other?

tools.name: Microsoft Teams
tools.source: Direct access — active, multiple channels including system monitoring alerts
tools.notes: OVERLAP: Both Teams AND Slack listed on engineering laptop setup page. Part of M365 licensing.
tools.interview_question: Both Teams and Slack are on the standard laptop setup. Is one primary and the other legacy, or do different teams use different tools?

tools.name: Slack
tools.source: Cake Engineering Tools page — listed as Yellow/Medium criticality, managed by IT team. Engineering Software page lists it for both Dev and QE laptops.
tools.pricing_notes: Free tier or Pro/Business+. If paid for all users, significant cost.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: Dual communication platform. If both are paid, this is a consolidation opportunity.

tools.name: Microsoft Forms
tools.source: Direct use — surveys built on MM account
tools.notes: Part of Microsoft 365 licensing.

tools.name: Keycloak
tools.source: AWS — dev-KeycloakServer-1 EC2 in CAKE Development
tools.notes: Self-hosted identity provider.

tools.name: Neo4j
tools.source: AWS — neo4j-2 EC2 instance in CAKE Development
tools.pricing_notes: Community vs Enterprise unclear from AWS scan alone.
tools.notes: Graph database. Community vs Enterprise edition unclear.
tools.interview_question: Is Neo4j on the community or enterprise edition? That's a meaningful cost difference.

tools.name: Kafka
tools.source: AWS — 2 Kafka instances (qa-data-platform-kafka1, kafka2) in CAKE Development. Confluence — DP Kafka Cluster Metric Exporting.
tools.pricing_notes: Apache OSS or Confluent (paid). Check if using Confluent add-ons.
tools.notes: Self-hosted on EC2. Active Kafka observability work (JMX + OpenTelemetry per Confluence).
tools.interview_question: Is this pure Apache Kafka or are you using any Confluent components?

tools.name: Elasticsearch
tools.source: AWS — 6 instances in CAKE Development (qa-data-platform-elastic-*), 5 instances in Retail Prod US (prod-elasticsearch01-05)
tools.estimated_annual_cost: $0 (OSS) + EC2 hosting (~$12,000–24,000/year for 11 instances) or Elastic subscription
tools.pricing_notes: 11 total instances across Dev and Prod. Self-hosted — check if OSS or Elastic subscription.
tools.notes: Significant footprint — 11 instances in 2 accounts.
tools.interview_question: 11 Elasticsearch instances across dev and prod — is this OSS or are you paying for an Elastic subscription?

tools.name: Amazon MQ (RabbitMQ)
tools.source: AWS Cost Explorer — $5,709/month across 3 regions (US: $2,909, EU: $1,694, APAC: $1,106). Also dev-rabbitmq-core02 in CAKE Dev.
tools.pricing_notes: Managed RabbitMQ. Largest single Retail line item.
tools.notes: Potential modernization opportunity. $68K/year for managed message broker.
tools.interview_question: Amazon MQ is $5,700/month across 3 regions. Has modernizing to SQS/SNS or EventBridge been considered?

tools.name: AWS CodeArtifact
tools.source: AWS — $12.71/month in Shared Artifact Registry account.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: Running alongside NpmJS and Archiva (legacy Maven repo). Consolidation opportunity.
tools.interview_question: CodeArtifact, NpmJS, and Archiva — are all three still needed, or can you consolidate?

tools.name: NpmJS
tools.source: Cake Engineering Tools page — listed as Yellow/Medium criticality, managed by Cloud services team.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: May be superseded by CodeArtifact.

tools.name: Archiva
tools.source: Cake Engineering Tools page — 'Archiva - Maven repository'. Red/High criticality. Managed by Cloud services team.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: Legacy Maven repo likely superseded by CodeArtifact.

tools.name: DockerHub
tools.source: Cake Engineering Tools page — Yellow/Medium criticality. Managed by Leads/Cloud services.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: Running alongside Amazon ECR ($16.53/mo in Shared Artifact Registry).
tools.interview_question: DockerHub vs ECR — which is the standard? Are you on a paid DockerHub plan?

tools.name: Backstage / TechDocs
tools.source: AWS inventory — mm-techdocs-storage S3 bucket in Shared Artifact Registry
tools.notes: Developer portal. If in use, indicates dev experience investment.

tools.name: n8n
tools.source: Confluence — 'AI ETL Workflows - n8n' in Concierge Team and Architecture spaces. Used for Smart Prompts, AI Summary Validation, Customer Analyzer.
tools.pricing_notes: Self-hosted or cloud. Used for AI ETL workflows connecting to OpenAI.
tools.notes: Active use for AI workflows. Fair-code licensed.
tools.interview_question: Is n8n self-hosted or on their cloud plan? It's driving several AI workflows.

tools.name: Figma
tools.source: Restaurant Update deck. Confluence — 'Design Integration with Cursor' uses Figma MCP server.
tools.pricing_notes: $15/editor/mo (Professional) or $75/editor/mo (Organization).
tools.notes: Confirmed by Figma MCP integration in Confluence.

tools.name: Salesforce
tools.source: AI space Tools page (under Sales). Cake Engineering Tools page — Yellow/Medium criticality, managed by SFDC team/Cloud services. Confluence — 'Existing Software (SaaS) Contracts'.
tools.pricing_notes: Enterprise/Unlimited editions.
tools.notes: Revenue.io integrates with Salesforce. 'Existing Software (SaaS) Contracts' page tracks entitlement processes.

tools.name: Five9
tools.source: AI space Tools page — listed under Customer Support, owner: Todd
tools.pricing_notes: Cloud contact center. Core/Premium/Optimum/Ultimate tiers.
tools.notes: Listed with Bland.AI as AI-enabled. May be one of the larger non-engineering SaaS costs. KEY ASK for Ana.
tools.interview_question: How many Five9 agent seats does the CS team run? What tier?

tools.name: Bland.AI
tools.source: AI space Tools page — listed as AI enhancement for Customer Support alongside Five9
tools.pricing_notes: AI phone agent. Usage-based pricing per call/minute.
tools.notes: AI-powered phone agent for CS. Paired with Five9.

tools.name: Revenue.io
tools.source: AI space Tools page — listed as AI enhancement for Sales alongside Salesforce
tools.pricing_notes: Revenue intelligence platform. Integrates with Salesforce.
tools.notes: AI-powered sales intelligence.

tools.name: Lovable
tools.source: Confluence — 'Agentic Lovable Dev Flow' (Cake Apps space, March 2026). 'Lovable Puls report components' whiteboard.
tools.pricing_notes: AI app builder. Used for rapid UI prototyping.
tools.notes: Active use for prototyping (Security Audit Log screen, Puls report components). Integrated with Cursor workflow.
tools.interview_question: The Agentic Lovable Dev Flow doc from March 2026 shows a sophisticated Cursor + Lovable + Chrome DevTools workflow. How widely adopted is this?

tools.name: Tyk API Gateway
tools.source: Confluence — Multiple license renewal pages (2022, 2023). 'TYK API Gateway Cost Breakdown' shows £25,000/year Pro Unlimited. ECS clusters running Tyk Dashboard, Gateway, Pump, Identity Broker.
tools.cost_detail: ~£25,000/year ($31,000/year) Pro Unlimited license + dev/test environments £1,500 each
tools.pricing_notes: Pro Unlimited license. Dev/test env: £1,500/env/year. Annual renewal.
tools.notes: Licensed API gateway with annual renewals. ECS-deployed (Dashboard, Gateway, Pump, Identity Broker). License last renewed 2023.
tools.interview_question: The Tyk license was last renewed in 2023 at ~$35K/year. Is it current?

tools.name: TeamViewer
tools.source: Cake Engineering Tools page — Yellow/Medium criticality. Managed by Ops team. 'Used to login to POS. Access is granted via Pulse.'
tools.pricing_notes: Remote access for POS devices. Business/Premium tiers.
tools.notes: POS-specific remote access through Pulse tunnel.

tools.name: SonarQube
tools.source: Confluence — Tooling Evaluation requires Snyk and SonarQube support. Release notes reference 'Sonar & Unit Tests' approvals.
tools.pricing_notes: Community (free) or Developer/Enterprise editions.
tools.notes: Referenced as a CI/CD requirement in Tooling Evaluation. Release approvals mention 'Sonar' checks.
tools.interview_question: SonarQube is referenced in release approvals. Is it on the free community edition or a paid tier?

tools.name: SpiraTest / QCenter
tools.source: Confluence — 'Spira - SUTAF Integration Guide'. QCenter referenced in multiple test execution reports.
tools.notes: Multiple test management tools alongside Jira. QCenter appears to be internal (qcenter.cake.net).
tools.interview_question: SpiraTest, QCenter, and Jira are all tracking test results. Is there a plan to consolidate?

tools.name: Domo
tools.source: Config references in Confluence — 'mm.analyticsUrl' points to Domo analytics. Used for Concierge analytics.
tools.pricing_notes: BI platform. Professional/Enterprise tiers.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: Running alongside AWS QuickSight (which has Finance dashboards and cost optimization dashboards).
tools.interview_question: Is Domo still the primary BI tool, or has QuickSight replaced it for some use cases?

tools.name: AWS QuickSight
tools.source: Confluence — 'Quicksight Dashboards' page. Finance Reporting Dashboard, CORA Dashboard, CUDOS Dashboard, Trusted Advisor Organization View.
tools.pricing_notes: $24/author/mo, $5/reader session. Used primarily for FinOps.
tools.notes: OVERLAP: BI alongside Domo. Finance Reporting Dashboard, CORA (Cost Optimization), CUDOS, Trusted Advisor dashboards.
